methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Sicona Battery Technologies is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Christiaan Jordaan and Andrew Minett. The valuation assessment draws on a universe of 9 private and 1 public comparable company across the battery and energy storage sector, including peers such as Graphyte, Fourth Power, Steady Energy, TAE Technologies, and Redwood Materials.

The peer-median method was applied using three same-stage, same-sector comparables. Graphyte trades at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, and Steady Energy at $110M. The median across these three yields $150M. This approach is most appropriate given Sicona's early-stage positioning and the availability of direct peers in the climate and energy infrastructure space. The resulting range spans $110M to $200M.

Given a single triangulation method, the analysis supports a valuation range of $128M to $172M, with a median implied value of $150M. Against Sicona's prior funding of $22M+, this implies a post-Series A multiple of approximately 6.8x. The key uncertainty in this assessment is reliance on a single valuation method and limited visibility into the specific stage and traction profiles of peer companies. A more robust estimate would benefit from revenue or technology-stage adjustments, as well as additional comparable methods if transaction data becomes available.
